title: Spot the brute-force
description:
Amaka drops a printout on your desk: Sankofa's SSH auth log from Q2, pulled off the jump host. 'Something pounded on our front door between 02:00 and 02:30 hitting the admin account. I need an exact number before escalation, not a hand-wave.'

Open the Log Viewer, switch to the auth.log tab, and use the regex filter to isolate failed SSH logins for user admin inside that half-hour window. Try a pattern like `Failed password for admin` and watch the line counter in the top right — that is your count. Click each matching line to drop it into the evidence scratchpad so Tunde has something to review.

Once you are confident in the count, scroll for the one sshd session entry Amaka left stamped in the log for you: a line beginning with `Accepted publickey for amaka` that carries a `session=TRAN{...}` token. That token is your flag. Submit it exactly as written.

widgetConfig.instructions: auth.log covers 2025-05-17 01:50 to 03:05 UTC on sankofa-bastion-01. Use the filter (regex, case-insensitive) to narrow the view; click a line to copy it to the scratchpad.
